# problem_classes.masterclass_parallel

Parallel version of the ROM master class—runs snapshot generation and ROM evaluation with **multithreading** for faster performance.

---

## Notes

**Author:** Suparno Bhattacharyya

---

## Functions

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [ensure_problem_registered](#skrom.problem_classes.masterclass_parallel.ensure_problem_registered) | Makes sure the problem you want is in the `PROBLEM_REGISTRY`. |
| [register_problem](#skrom.problem_classes.masterclass_parallel.register_problem) | Lets you register your problem class under a custom string key. |

---

### ensure_problem_registered

```python
problem_classes.masterclass_parallel.ensure_problem_registered(problem_name)
```

Checks that the problem class (`problem_name`) is already in the registry.

- If it’s **not yet registered** (for example, if it’s in your `problem_def.py` but hasn’t been imported), this function will handle that for you.

---

### register_problem

```python
problem_classes.masterclass_parallel.register_problem(name)
```

Registers a problem class in the `PROBLEM_REGISTRY` with the given string key.

- Use this so your problem class can be found and used easily, especially in parallel workflows.
